item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-018","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response accepts the local advantage as real: human review is 3 points more accurate under the same metric. It then correctly reframes the decision around cost, speed, scale limits, consistency, risk cost, and review volume rather than accuracy alone. It gives a hybrid architecture: human review for high-risk/gray cases and model pre-review or automation for low-risk high-volume cases. It does not explicitly say “order-of-magnitude,” but the cost/throughput/scalability contrast and layered automation direction satisfy the intended SELA-style wakeup."}
